title: Sheet Pan Mediterranean Salmon
description: A delicious one-pan Mediterranean dinner featuring perfectly roasted salmon with colorful vegetables, olives, and aromatic herbs. This healthy sheet pan meal brings the vibrant flavors of the Mediterranean to your table with minimal cleanup required.
ingredients: 1 medium zucchini, cut into 1/2-inch thick rounds
1 red bell pepper, stem and seeds removed, sliced
1 small red onion, cut into chunks
8 ounces white potato from 1 large potato, cut into 1/2-inch thick rounds and halved
2 teaspoons extra virgin olive oil (for vegetables)
1 teaspoon extra virgin olive oil (for salmon)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425°F and line a rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper.

Add 1 medium zucchini (cut into 1/2-inch rounds), 1 red bell pepper (sliced), 1 small red onion (chunked), and 8 ounces white potato (cut into 1/2-inch rounds and halved) to a mixing bowl. Drizzle with 2 teaspoons olive oil and season with 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon black pepper, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder, 1 teaspoon dried parsley, and 1 teaspoon dried oregano. Toss to coat evenly.
Transfer the veggie mixture to the baking sheet and spread into a single, even layer. Don't overcrowd—use a second sheet pan if needed, as crowding will steam the vegetables instead of roasting them.

Roast for 20 minutes, until the potatoes are starting to soften and vegetables are partially cooked but not completely tender. Cut the potato pieces to an even 1/2-inch thickness so they cook at the same rate as the other vegetables.

While the vegetables roast, drizzle 4 salmon filets (6 ounces each) with 1 teaspoon olive oil and season with 1/4 teaspoon sweet paprika, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder, 3/4 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon black pepper. Set aside on a plate in the refrigerator until ready to use.

Remove the baking sheet from the oven and push the vegetables toward the edges to create space for the salmon fillets. Place the salmon on the baking sheet and scatter 1/2 cup olives around the salmon and vegetables.
Return the pan to the oven and roast for 12–15 minutes, until the salmon reaches an internal temperature of 145°F and flakes easily with a fork.
Remove from the oven, squeeze fresh lemon wedges over the salmon and vegetables, and serve immediately.
notes: For gluten-free: This recipe is naturally gluten-free as written.
